meta.note: Quantitative claims extracted from body, figures (esp. Figs 5, 6), and experimental section. Several headline numbers (14% PLQY, 6.84 µs lifetime, 1% doping) appear redundantly in abstract, results, and conclusions.

claims.text: Photoluminescence quantum yields up to 14% on quartz were obtained for the doped wide-bandgap perovskite.

location.quote:
Photoluminescence quantum yields
(up to 14% on quartz), as well as photoluminescence lifetimes (up to ~7 µs on quartz),
indicate improved optical properties.

claims.predicate: reported_max_PLQY_pct == 14 on quartz, consistent with figure/text value
claims.scope: doped Cs0.21FA0.74MA0.05Pb(I0.81Br0.14Cl0.05)3 thin film on quartz, 1% doping

claims.text: Photoluminescence lifetimes up to ~7 µs on quartz indicate improved optical properties.

location.quote: photoluminescence lifetimes (up to ~7 µs on quartz)

claims.predicate: reported_max_PL_lifetime_us ~ 7 on quartz, consistent with Figure 6 value (6.84 µs)

claims.text: The optimal total doping concentration of Zn(II) and formate is 1% relative to Pb(II).

location.quote:
the optimal
total doping concentration of zinc ions (Zn(II)) and formate anions (Fo−) is determined to
be 1% relative to Pb(II).

claims.predicate: optimal_total_doping_pct == 1 relative to Pb(II), with Zn 0.5% + Fo 0.5%

claims.text: PCE of single-junction cells is capped at 33.7% by the Shockley–Queisser limit and realistically limited to 29.4% for silicon considering Auger recombination.

location.quote:
capped at 33.7% by the Shockley–Queisser (SQ) limit [1]
and, considering Auger recombination [2], realistically limited to 29.4% for silicon (Si).

claims.text: Tandem solar cells theoretically enable PCEs up to ~45%.

location.quote: theoretically enabling PCEs up to ~45% [3]

claims.text: The current certified world record obtained by LONGi stands at 35.0% PCE.

location.quote: The current certified world record obtained by LONGi stands at 35.0% PCE

claims.text: Mariotti et al. achieved a certified efficiency of 32.5% in triple-halide perovskite–silicon tandem solar cells in 2022.

location.quote:
Mariotti et al. [6] achieved a certified efficiency of 32.5% in
triple-halide perovskite–silicon tandem solar cells in 2022.

claims.text: Jeong et al. demonstrated that addition of 2% formate anions increases cell efficiency to 25.2%.

location.quote:
the addition of 2% (Fo−) formate anions
significantly increases cell efficiency to 25.2%

claims.text: The current world record (27.2% PCE) contains potassium binoxalate at 0.7 mol%, 1.5 mg/mL.

location.quote:
the current
world record (27.2% PCE) [31] contains potassium binoxalate

claims.text: Asymmetrically substituted carbazole HTL resulted in a Si tandem cell with 34.58% PCE (certified).

location.quote:
resulting in a Si
tandem cell with 34.58% PCE (certified)

claims.text: A maximum PLQY of 14% was obtained for wide-bandgap perovskite on quartz, while Mariotti et al. reported 3.75% PLQY for their films on quartz.

location.quote:
a maximum PLQY of 14% was
obtained for wide-bandgap perovskite on quartz

claims.text: Mariotti et al. reported 3.75% PLQY and a PL lifetime of ~2 µs (by tail-fit) for their films on quartz.

location.quote:
Mariotti et al. [6] reported 3.75% as PLQY for their films on
quartz (and a PL lifetime of ~2 µs, by tail-fit)

claims.scope: Mariotti et al. [6] films on quartz

claims.text: The 2025 material shows a PL lifetime of 6.84 ± 1.1 µs, compared to 3.65 ± 0.8 µs (2024) and 1.57 ± 0.1 µs (2019).

location.quote:
the red
trace shows our 2025 material with a lifetime of 6.84 ± 1.1 µs, compared to 3.65 ± 0.8 µs
(2024, blue) and 1.57 ± 0.1 µs (2019, green)

claims.predicate: lifetime_2025_us == 6.84 ; lifetime_2024_us == 3.65 ; lifetime_2019_us == 1.57, consistent with Figure 6
claims.scope: TR-PL of three perovskite films, Figure 6

claims.text: Beta values from stretched exponential fitting are 0.90 (1.57 µs), 0.77 (3.65 µs), and 0.84 (6.84 µs).

location.quote:
Beta values are, respectively, 0.90 (1.57 µs) [9], 0.77 (3.65 µs) [11], and 0.84 (6.84 µs),
obtained with stretched exponential fitting.

claims.predicate: beta values: 0.90, 0.77, 0.84 paired with lifetimes 1.57, 3.65, 6.84 µs

claims.text: The perovskite crystals have a dimension of ca. 400 nm.

location.quote:
The perovskite crystals have a dimension of
ca. 400 nm (see Figure 4—left).

claims.text: Laminated 1% doped optimized films show no yellowing after more than 1 year, but partial laser irradiation (532 nm) showed photodegradation after 500 hrs.

location.quote:
laser irradiation with 532 nm through a pinhole
or a slit (=partial illumination) showed clear signs of permanent photodegradation after
500 hrs

claims.predicate: photodegradation observed after 500 hrs at 532 nm, 1-5 suns

claims.text: Mariotti et al. reported 25% loss after 478 h of illumination for undoped material.

location.quote:
Mariotti et al. reported 25% loss after 478 h
of illumination (for “undoped” material) [6]

claims.text: The conclusion restates a PLQY of 14% and PL lifetime of 6.84 µs obtained at 1% total doping.

location.quote:
A PLQY
of 14% has been obtained and a PL lifetime of 6.84 µs.

claims.predicate: conclusion_PLQY_pct == 14 and conclusion_lifetime_us == 6.84, consistent with results

claims.text: Perovskite precursor solutions were 1.4 M, prepared in 600 µL DMF and 150 µL DMSO.

location.quote:
precursor solutions (1.4 M) were
prepared by dissolving FAI, MACl, CsI, PbI2, PbBr2, and PbCl2 in 600 µL DMF and 150 µL
DMSO

claims.text: Films were spin-coated at 5000 rpm for 45 s with 300 µL anisole dropped at 25 s, and annealed at 100 °C for 20 min.

location.quote:
The perovskite
films were spin-coated at 5000 rpm for 45 s (5 s acceleration), with 300 µL anisole dropped
at 25 s after the start of spin-coating, and annealed on a hot plate at 100 °C for 20 min.
